user_prompt: Draw trapezoid MDSG where M is top-left, D is top-right, S is bottom-right, and G is bottom-left. Mark MD as the top base and GS as the bottom base, with arrows showing MD is parallel to GS. Draw the midsegment JE connecting those two midpoints and mark it as parallel to both bases with arrows. Mark J as the midpoint of MG with tick marks, and mark E as the midpoint of DS with tick marks. Also draw an auxiliary point T by extending side MD and drawing a diagonal from G through D to hit the extension at T — this creates the triangle used in the triangle midsegment theorem proof. Label all points M, D, S, G, J, E, and T.
